title: 1.1 Explains how parallel processing limits concurrent deliveries to 16 (e.g., buffered channel semaphore, worker pool, goroutine limit, or similar concurrency mechanism)
1.2 Reports initial delivery time of approximately 64 seconds for 500 messages with 16 parallel workers at 2 seconds each
1.3 Reports total time until bounce of approximately 63-64 hours using delays 15m, 30m, 1h, 2h, 4h, 8h, 16h, 32h
1.4 Explains that TriesCount equals zero produces exponent negative one yielding 2^(-1) equals 0.5
1.5 Explains why TriesCount equals zero does not occur because increment happens before delay calculation
1.6 Reports go test command execution on internal/target/queue with passing results
1.7 Reports retry delay formula initialRetryTime times retryTimeScale to the power of TriesCount minus one with values 15min, scale 2, maxTries 8
1.8 Reports that 9 total delivery attempts occur before bounce when maxTries equals 8
2.1 Claims that exponential backoff has a maximum delay cap limiting individual retry intervals
